raw_text:
Start some Phase 18 in parallel — but only the ones that are pure scaffolding / pure CI plumbing and won’t churn the Phase 17 foundations.

Correct strategy

Do option 2 with guardrails:

Run Phase 17 as the critical path

In parallel, start the Phase 18 beads that are:

low coupling

low merge-conflict risk

don’t force design decisions that Phase 17 will influence

✅ Start now (parallel-safe)
1) bd-18s0 — Scaffold platform/security

This is equivalent to your Phase 17 scaffolding beads: low risk, no coupling if kept pure.
Rule: crate only, placeholders only, no enforcement yet.

2) bd-18s1 — Scaffold tools/compliance-export

Same: create binary crate + workspace wiring + prints help.

These two are safe because they’re isolated and will not collide with Phase 17 core work.

⚠️ Start now only if you freeze scope tightly
3) bd-18a0 — Release pipeline

You can start it, but restrict it to artifact build + checksum + attach artifact and nothing more.
Avoid anything that depends on:

schema/projection version conventions (Phase 17 will define some of this),

environment promotion semantics,

signing/attestation choices.

If you keep bd-18a0 to “build all workspace crates, produce artifacts + SHA256 manifest” it’s safe. If you try to solve full promotion semantics now, you’ll rework it after Phase 17.

4) bd-18a1 — Config validation

Same story: safe if it’s only a contract doc + minimal failfast harness.
But if it touches service startup flows while Phase 17 is adding new crates and tools, you’ll create conflicts. I’d hold bd-18a1 until bd-18a0 lands cleanly.

❌ Do not start yet

Everything else in Phase 18 should wait because it depends on Phase 17’s implementation details:

service-to-service auth depends on fallback + tenantctl patterns

RBAC depends on tenant lifecycle + rebuild tooling decisions

DR drill depends on rebuild + audit oracle

rate limiting depends on fallback behavior

compliance export depends on audit + tenant registry schemas

Practical sequencing (what I’d actually run)

Wave 0 (now):

Phase 17 scaffolding: bd-17s0/s1/s2 (already planned)

Phase 18 scaffolding: bd-18s0 + bd-18s1

Wave 1 (as soon as Phase 17 scaffolding merges):

bd-17a0, bd-17b0, bd-17c0 (critical path)

bd-18a0 can run if you lock it to “artifact build + checksum manifest”

That gets you parallel throughput without destabilizing the Phase 17 truth-at-scale work.

If you want a hard rule: parallelize only beads whose files don’t overlap and whose outputs don’t constrain Phase 17 design.
